# Matt Whalley

> AI Product Engineer bridging AI, product, design, and engineering to turn emerging technology into products people can actually use.

Matt Whalley is an AI Product Engineer based in St. Augustine, Florida. He leads UX engineering and frontend architecture for AI-enabled web and mobile products, with a focus on making AI useful, usable, and sustainable. He works across the full product stack — from design systems and component architecture to agentic workflows and AI-assisted development tooling.

## Focus Areas

- AI product engineering and agentic development workflows
- Frontend architecture (React, React Native, Next.js)
- Design systems and design-to-code handoff
- UX engineering and user-centered product development
- Technical leadership and team enablement

## Selected Work

- [AI Product Development at DataSpring](https://dataspring.com) — Leading UX engineering and frontend architecture for AI-enabled web and mobile initiatives. React interfaces, AI-assisted development workflows, design system standards, and secure authentication patterns.
- [Can I Surf Today?](https://canisurf.today) — Hyperlocal AI-powered surf report. Gathers realtime buoy, tide, and weather data and produces a surf report via AI. Built with Next.js, Vercel AI SDK, NOAA/Open-Meteo APIs, Neon, and Bun.
- [HANG LAB](https://testflight.apple.com/join/FTcb21dX) — iOS/WatchOS hangboard training app for climbers, built in Swift with HealthKit, WatchKit, SiriKit, and haptics.
- [RFD Checker](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1621901729405123866) — Figma plugin that audits a selected frame against a Ready-for-Dev checklist. Checks design tokens, component mapping, layout, handoff hygiene, and accessibility. Built for teams shipping from Figma to component-based codebases, with first-class Material UI support.

## Experiments & Open Source

Business Bingo, D U S T (writing tool), Grounded, Sui Sans (typeface), Vinyly, Photobooth, Short Scories, and several Figma plugins (Chart Generator, Design Standards Checker, Get Layer Text).

GitHub: https://github.com/mttwhlly
